rulings.Case Title: JAMES AUSTIN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Mandatory Or Discretionary Relief Per Ccp 473b From Order Striking Reply To Plaintiffs Opposition To Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 15, 2019, LINE 13 DEFENDANTS RENKA PROP LLC, JULIA MWANGI, and ANNE KIHAGI'S Motion For Mandatory Or Discretionary Relief From Order Striking Reply To Plaintiffs Opposition To Motion For Summary Judgment, Per Section 473b Code of Civil Procedure GRANTED in part. The Court will consider Defendants' late-filed reply brief, but will not consider the voluminous additional documentary evidence improperly included with that brief, which Defendants' counsel's declaration fails to explain or justify. "The general rule of motion practice, which applies here, is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers. This principle is most prominent in the context of summary judgment motions, which is not surprising, given that it is a common evidentiary motion. '[T]he inclusion of additional evidentiary matter with the reply should only be allowed in the exceptional case . . . . .'" (Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537 .) The Court denies Defendants' request to schedule a second hearing on Defendants' motion for summary judgment. The Court has considered the late-filed reply brief, which does not alter the Court's view of the merits of Defendants' motion for summary judgment or alternatively for summary adjudication. The Court will not reconsider its March 19, 2019 order denying Defendants' motion. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions in their opposition brief is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ROSA RODRIGUEZ VS. GEAR UP COMMUNICATIONS INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition Appearance
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2019, Line 1, DEFENDANT GEAR UP COMMUNICATIONS INC.'s Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Deposition Appearance Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Gear Up Communications' unopposed Motion to Compel Plaintiff's Deposition Appearance is GRANTED. Plaintiff shall be presented for her deposition within one week of notice of entry of this order. If plaintiff fails to appear for her deposition within one week of notice of entry of this order, sanctions in the amount of $2,234.20 shall be due against plaintiff and her counsel within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO INDEPENDENT TAXI ASSOCIATION VS. ROGER CARDENAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 15, 2019, LINE 5 DEFENDANT ROGER CARDENAS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/FOR LEAVE TO DEFEND Defendant Roger Cardenas, dba Bay Cab and Arrow Checker Cab's motion to vacate default and to quash service of summons is granted. Although Plaintiff San Francisco Independent Taxi Association acted with "reasonable diligence" in attempting to serve Defendant, Plaintiff has not demonstrated that the individual who received the summons and complaint was the person apparently in charge of Defendant's "usual place of business." (Code Civ. Proc. § 415.20(b).) Defendant shows that he has only a tenuous relationship with National Cab Company at 2270 McKinnon Avenue in that he lodges his taxi medallion with them, but does not maintain a desk or office space at that address. (Cardenas Decl. 4.) Moreover, Plaintiff has not shown that the person who received substitute service is someone whose "relationship with the person to be served makes it more likely than not that they will deliver process to the named party." (Bein v. Brechtel-Jochim Group, Inc. (1992) 6 Cal.App.4th 1387, 1393.) Plaintiff has only shown that the summons and complaint were left with an unnamed dispatcher. Given Defendant's representations that he does not know "John Doe," that he did not receive actual notice of service, and that his illness prevented him from personally accepting service at an earlier time, the Court concludes that substitute service was not properly effectuated. Therefore, service of summons is quashed. (Code Civ. Proc. § 418.10.) As a result, the Court was without jurisdiction when it entered default against Defendant on August 9, 2018, and that default is vacated as void pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d). (See Strathvale Holdings v. E.B.H. (2005) 126 Cal.App.4th 1241, 1249.) Plaintiff San Francisco Independent Taxi Association's motion for default judgment shall be taken off calendar. Defendant's sworn declaration indicates that he had serious health issues until August 2018. Defendant is therefore ordered to accept service of Plaintiff's complaint at his home address of 34 Liberty Street, #2 or through other means agreed upon by the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: KAREN CLOPTON VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Discovery Or In The Alternative For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, May 15, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANTs CALIFORNIA PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION, MICHAEL PICKER, and LIANE RANDOLPH's Motion To Stay Discovery Or In The Alternative For A Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants California Public Utilities Commission, Picker, and Randolph's Motion to Stay Discovery or, in the Alternative, for a Protective Order is DENIED. Defendants move to stay discovery "pursuant to the Court's inherent power to stay proceedings." Defendants cite to Wise v. Pac. Gas & Elec. Co.(1999) 77 Cal. App. 4th 287, 295 and other cases. The cases cited generally relate to a stay of an entire action, not a stay of discovery. The Court declines defendants' invitation to exercise its inherent power to stay discovery in this action. Defendants move in the alternative for a protective order. Defendants cite to CCP Sections 2025.420 (b)(9)-(10) and 2031.060(b)(1). CCP Section 2025.420 relates to depositions. It is unclear how this Section is applicable. CCP Section 2031.060 relates to document requests. However, defendants failed to make an adequate showing of good cause that the production would cause "unwarranted annoyance, embarrassment, or oppression, or undue burden and expense" to support a motion for protective order. CCP Section 2031.060(b). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcatalanotti@fmglaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION VS. A&J MANUFACTURING, L.L.C. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Blue Rhino Global Sourcing, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 15, 2019, LINE 6 PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of Consent Judgment As To Defendant Blue Rhino Global Sourcing, Inc. GRANTED. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). The Court will sign the proposed order and consent judgment included with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
